segments.text: Lesson 42.

translations.zh-Hans: 第42课。

segments.text: Modern Cavemen.

translations.zh-Hans: 现代洞穴人。

segments.text: Listen to the tape, then answer the question below.

translations.zh-Hans: 听录音，然后回答以下问题。

segments.text: With what does the writer compare the Guffa Burge?

translations.zh-Hans: 作者把古弗尔·贝尔热洞比作什么？

segments.text: Cave exploration, or potholing as it has come to be known, is a relatively new sport.

translations.zh-Hans: 洞穴探险，也就是如今所说的地下洞穴探险，是一项较新的运动。

segments.text: Perhaps it is the desire for solitude, or the chance of making an unexpected discovery that lures people down to the depths of the earth.

translations.zh-Hans: 也许是对独处的渴望，或意外发现某物的机会，把人们吸引到地球深处。

segments.text: It is impossible to give a satisfactory explanation for a potholder's motives.

translations.zh-Hans: 要对洞穴探险者的动机作出令人满意的解释是不可能的。

segments.text: For him, have the same peculiar fascination which high mountains have for the climber.

translations.zh-Hans: 对他来说，洞穴具有高山对登山者同样独特的吸引力。

segments.text: They arouse instincts which can only be dimly understood.

translations.zh-Hans: 它们唤起的本能只能得到模糊的理解。

segments.text: Exploring really deep caves is not a task for the Sunday afternoon rambler.

translations.zh-Hans: 探索真正深邃的洞穴，并非星期日下午散步的人所能胜任。

segments.text: Such undertakings require the precise planning and foresight of military operations.

translations.zh-Hans: 这类行动需要像军事行动一样精确的计划和预见。

segments.text: It can take as long as eight days to rig up rope ladders and to establish supply bases before a descent can be made into a very deep cave.

translations.zh-Hans: 在进入一个很深的洞穴之前，架设绳梯和建立补给基地可能要花整整八天。

segments.text: Precautions of this sort are necessary, for it is impossible to foretell the exact nature of the difficulties which will confront the potholder.

translations.zh-Hans: 这种预防措施必不可少，因为无法预知洞穴探险者究竟会遇到什么困难。

segments.text: The deepest known cave in the world is the Guffa Burge near Grenoble.

translations.zh-Hans: 世界上已知最深的洞穴是格勒诺布尔附近的古弗尔·贝尔热洞。

segments.text: It extends to a depth of 3,723 feet.

translations.zh-Hans: 它深达3723英尺。

segments.text: This immense chasm has been formed by an underground stream which has tunnelled a course through a flaw in the rocks.

translations.zh-Hans: 这道巨大的深渊由一条地下河形成，河水沿岩石的一处裂隙冲出了通道。

segments.text: The entrance to the cave is on a plateau in the Dauphiné Alps.

translations.zh-Hans: 洞口位于多菲内阿尔卑斯山的一处高原上。

segments.text: As it is only six feet across, it is barely noticeable.

translations.zh-Hans: 它只有6英尺宽，几乎无法察觉。

segments.text: The cave might never have been discovered had not the entrance been spotted by the distinguished French potholder Burge.

translations.zh-Hans: 若不是杰出的法国洞穴探险家贝尔热发现了洞口，这个洞也许永远不会被发现。

segments.text: Since its discovery, it has become a sort of potholder's Everest.

translations.zh-Hans: 自发现以来，它已成为洞穴探险者心目中的珠穆朗玛峰。

segments.text: Though a number of descents have been made, much of it still remains to be explored.

translations.zh-Hans: 虽然人们已经多次下洞探险，但其中许多地方仍有待探索。

segments.text: A team of potholers recently went down the Guffa Burge.

translations.zh-Hans: 最近，一支洞穴探险队深入了古弗尔·贝尔热洞。

segments.text: After entering the narrow gap on the plateau, they climbed down the steep sides of the cave until they came to a narrow corridor.

translations.zh-Hans: 他们从高原上的狭窄洞口进入，沿陡峭洞壁下行，来到一条狭窄通道。

segments.text: They had to edge their way along this, sometimes wading across shallow streams or swimming across deep pools.

translations.zh-Hans: 他们不得不侧身沿通道前进，时而蹚过浅溪，时而游过深潭。

segments.text: Suddenly they came to a waterfall which dropped into an underground lake at the bottom of the cave.

translations.zh-Hans: 突然，他们来到一道瀑布前，瀑布直泻洞底的地下湖。

segments.text: They plunged into the lake, and after loading their gear on an inflatable rubber dinghy, let the current carry them to the other side.

translations.zh-Hans: 他们跳进湖中，把装备装到充气橡皮艇上，任水流将他们带到对岸。

segments.text: To protect themselves from the icy water, they had to wear special rubber suits.

translations.zh-Hans: 为抵御冰冷的湖水，他们必须穿特制的橡胶服。

segments.text: At the far end of the lake, they came to huge piles of rubble which had been washed up by the water.

translations.zh-Hans: 在湖的另一端，他们遇到被水冲来的大堆碎石。

segments.text: In this part of the cave, they could hear an insistent booming sound, which they found was caused by a small waterspout shooting down into a pool from the roof of the cave.

translations.zh-Hans: 在洞穴的这部分，他们听到持续的轰鸣声，后来发现是洞顶一股小水柱射入水潭造成的。

segments.text: Squeezing through a cleft in the rocks, the potholers arrived at an enormous cavern the size of a huge concert hall.

translations.zh-Hans: 洞穴探险者挤过岩石间的一条裂缝，来到一个如大型音乐厅般宽阔的巨大洞窟。

segments.text: After switching on powerful arc lights, they saw great stalagmites, some of them over 40 feet high, rising up like tree trunks to meet the stalactites suspended from the roof.

translations.zh-Hans: 打开强力弧光灯后，他们看到巨大的石笋像树干般拔地而起，与洞顶垂下的钟乳石相接，有些高达40多英尺。

segments.text: Round about, piles of limestone glistened in all the colors of the rainbow.

translations.zh-Hans: 四周成堆的石灰岩闪耀着彩虹般的各种颜色。

segments.text: In the eerie silence of the cavern, the only sound that could be heard was made by water, which dripped continuously from the high dome above them.

translations.zh-Hans: 在洞窟诡异的寂静中，唯一能听到的是水从头顶高高的穹顶不断滴下的声音。
